per_turn_leaks.student_message: Worsening dyspnea with a productive cough — that combination raises concern. You mentioned fever. With fever and productive cough, have you had any night sweats or chills?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I did experience some night sweats - I'd wake up in the middle of the night feeling really hot and would even sweat through my pajamas. It's been happening for a few nights now, but like everything else, it seems to be getting worse over time.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Night sweats with fever and productive cough — that's a classic infectious triad. With respiratory infections, pleuritic involvement is common. Have you had any chest pain, especially when breathing deeply or coughing?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yes, I have been experiencing some right-sided chest pain, sharp and worse with deep breathing and coughing. It's not constant, but it's definitely more noticeable when I'm feeling short of breath.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Right-sided pleuritic pain fits with a lower lobe process. You mentioned allergic rhinitis — allergies and asthma often co-occur. Have you ever had wheezing episodes before this illness? Any history of asthma?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, I have experienced recurrent wheezing episodes since childhood, around age 8, but they've always been associated with colds or exercise, and never formally diagnosed. They would usually happen a couple times per year, and my parents just thought it was related to allergies like mine. But this time feels different - the cough and shortness of breath are much worse than any wheezing I've had before.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Your mother having asthma strengthens the genetic link. Speaking of family history — any other respiratory or serious conditions in the family?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My maternal grandmother passed away from lung cancer a few years ago, but I'm not sure if that's related to anything else going on with me right now.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: With this level of illness — fever, productive cough, night sweats, chest pain — your body is fighting hard. How has your appetite been? Any weight changes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I have to admit, I haven't had much of an appetite over the past few days. The fatigue and shortness of breath make it really hard for me to eat a full meal. As for weight changes, I've noticed that I've lost a bit of weight since this started - probably because I just can't keep anything down.
